mechanism_narrative: An increase in the monthly Industrial Production Index indicates expanding manufacturing output, which raises corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positively correlated with month-over-month changes in INDPRO (Spearman rank correlation p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.5%, elevated joblessness curtails household disposable income and compresses aggregate demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 6.5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in real retail sales and a decline in the unemployment rate signal robust demand-side momentum and tightening labor markets, jointly amplifying corporate earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and falling UNRATE fails to outperform the simple univariate RSAFS signal by at least 2 percentage points annualized over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term has a t-statistic below 1.5 in a linear projection of 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary easing cycles, month-over-month increases in housing starts signal successful transmission of accommodative policy to the real economy, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 1%, month-over-month increases in HOUST do not produce significantly positive 60-day S&P 500 returns compared to months with flat or falling HOUST (difference in mean returns t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index reduces expected real corporate earnings and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in CPIAUCSL are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP signals expansion in aggregate demand and national income, lifting corporate revenue baselines and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive quarterly growth in GDPC1 does not yield positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of quarters over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is expanding while the federal funds rate exceeds 4%, restrictive monetary policy interacts with supply-side heat to choke off future growth, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of rising INDPRO and FEDFUNDS > 4% fails to produce negative 20-day returns in at least 60% of triggered months, or the interaction term in a linear projection has a positive coefficient (t-stat > 1.5) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in nonfarm payroll employment signals rising household income and forthcoming consumption expenditure, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in PAYEMS are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.
